my.actor.lib.ArrayMinimum All Implemented Interfaces: Actor , java.lang.Cloneable, Debuggable , DebugListener , Executable , ModelErrorHandler , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor public class ArrayMinimum extends Transformer Extract the min

s ArrayMinimum extends Transformer Extract the minimum element from an array. This actor reads an array from the input port and sends the largest of its elements to the output port. The index of the smallest element (closest to minus infinity) is s

smallest element (closest to minus infinity) is sent to the index output port. If there is more than one entry in the array with the minimum value, then the index of the first such entry is what is produced. Since: Ptolemy II 3.0.2 Version: $Id: Arr

public ArrayMinimum ( CompositeEntity container, java.lang.String name) throws NameDuplicationException , IllegalActionException Construct an actor with the given container and name. Parameters: container - The contai

ainer and name. Parameters: container - The container. name - The name of this actor. Throws: IllegalActionException - If the actor cannot be contained by the proposed container. NameDuplicationException - If the container already has an actor

eption - If the container already has an actor with this name. Method Detail fire public void fire () throws IllegalActionException Consume at most one array from the input port and produce the minimum of its elements on the output por

ce the minimum of its elements on the output port and the index of that element on the index port. If there is no token on the input, then no output is produced. Specified by: fire in interface Executable Overrides: fire in class AtomicActor T

Executable Overrides: fire in class AtomicActor Throws: IllegalActionException - If there is no director. typeConstraintList public java.util.List typeConstraintList () Return the type constraints of this actor. In this class, the constraints are

this actor. In this class, the constraints are that the type of the input port is an array type, and the type of the output port is no less than the type of the elements of the input array. Specified by: typeConstraintList in interface TypedAc
